element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Basic attack, max distance screen height, always available
Powerful area-of-effect weapon, stress relief mechanic, collected via B icons
Color-coded weapon switching (red, blue, green, yellow), appears through destroyed carriers
Increases helicopter's base firepower, lost on death
Star-shaped items granting 3000-point bonus, optional scoring
Scattered pickups, awarded at point thresholds
Game loops with increased difficulty, denser projectile patterns

typical_tension_triggers: Continuous scrolling background (scenery never stops moving)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Constant vertical scrolling maintains forward momentum; tension builds through enemy density and difficulty; relief provided by bomb mechanics and pickups; ten increasingly difficult levels create macro tension curve

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Gold medals and 1-ups scattered throughout levels as optional collectibles

difficulty_curve.early_game: Standard enemy waves, basic shot and bomb introduction, accessible gameplay
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Four power-up weapon types introduced, player firepower enhancement available, moderate enemy density
difficulty_curve.late_game: Increasingly difficult level encounters, stronger boss battles, denser enemy formations
notable_spikes: Boss encounters at each level's end
Loop 2 completion: game restarts with significantly denser bullet patterns and increased difficulty

notable_level_patterns.description: Each of 10 levels ends with a mandatory boss fight before progression; vertical scrolling background continuously moves until helipad is reached
Enemy carriers destroyed to release color-coded weapon icons (red, blue, green, yellow) allowing weapon switching; maintains offensive engagement
After completing all 10 levels, game restarts (Loop 2) with same level structure but denser enemy bullet patterns and increased enemy toughness

sources_used: wikipedia: Twin Cobra article - gameplay section, development section
